codeReview.summary: The feature adds the Settings tab and a substantial management UI, but edit flows are functionally broken because edit form bindings read from immutable profile snapshots instead of mutable form state, preventing actual edits from sticking.

issues.description: Edit form bindings use getters like `get: { profile.displayName }` (and equivalent for identifier/CLI fields), while setters write to `@State` vars. Because getters never read those state vars, typed edits are immediately overwritten by the original profile values, so profile editing is not actually operable.
Error state is captured (`@State private var error`) and assigned in load/set-active/delete failures, but never rendered in the view. This weakens the "recoverable" UX expectation for failure paths.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker marked `skillFeedback.followedProcedure` true, but required switcher-ui-worker validation steps were not fully evidenced.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Skill requires `scripts/test-openburnbar-app.sh` and manual UI sanity checks (.factory/skills/switcher-ui-worker/SKILL.md lines 30-33). Handoff verification only lists xcodebuild commands (handoff JSON `verification.commandsRun` at lines 14-27) and includes no manual-check evidence.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Handoff commit ID integrity appears inconsistent with git history, creating review friction.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff reports commitId `45d1861b3a5e9c0e89d7f8a7b4e6d2c3a1f5e9b7`, but `git show` on that ID fails with `fatal: bad object`; actual commit is `45d186134278c9a630ed245b65c3ba168cc83a3d`.

summary: Reviewed feature settings-account-switcher-management-ui by inspecting mission metadata, handoff, transcript skeleton, skill contract, and commit diff. Result is FAIL due to a blocking edit-form binding defect that prevents profile updates from persisting in the Settings UI.
